title: Lentil & Mushroom Ragù
intro: Tom Kerridge proves lighter food can be deeply satisfying. This meat-free lentil and mushroom ragù is rich and hearty — the Thermomix builds it all in one bowl.
steps: Chop onion, garlic, carrot and mushrooms 5 sec / 0°C / speed 5. Scrape.
Add oil and sauté 4 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add lentils, tomato paste, passata, wine and oregano. Cook 28 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1, MC off and basket on the lid, until the lentils are tender. Season and serve over pasta with parmesan.
tips: Chopping the mushrooms fine gives the ragù a meaty texture.
Even better the next day — make a double batch.

faq.a: No — green lentils cook from dry in the sauce in about 28 minutes.

faq.q: Is this the original Tom Kerridge recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Tom Kerridge and lentil ragù; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Lentil & Mushroom Ragù in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Tom Kerridge-style lentil ragù: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
